title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, part of 160 events across Massachusetts.
The protest featured signs and displays with slogans like 'No Kings Then 1776; No Kings Now 2026.'
Demonstrators and photographers captured the rally, highlighting the event as part of a nationwide movement.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: The 'No Kings' movement held protests in Europe, including Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam, on March 28, 2026.
Over 3,000 'No Kings' protests planned across the U.S., with significant participation expected in Massachusetts and California.
Protesters held anti-Donald Trump placards, criticizing his policies and second presidential term since January 2025.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
Cast members include Alicia Carmody, a Providence restaurant owner, and Liz McGraw, a cannabis business owner.
The show explores Rhode Island's tight-knit social scene and features feuds, friendships, and family dynamics.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff Nick Cocchi announces cutting 50 positions to save up to $4 million after budget cuts.
Layoffs affect patrols in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, Palmer, and three analyst roles in Chicopee.
Sheriff's department attributes budget issues to historical legislative deficit-driven funding post-1999 county government abolition.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy officials approved $2.65 million for new PFAS-free firefighting gear after debates on safety and firefighter health.
More than 270 firefighters will receive two sets of PFAS-free gear, addressing contamination concerns linked to cancer risks.
Legislation signed in 2024 will outlaw selling firefighting gear with PFAS in Massachusetts starting January 2026.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, co-founder of The Eagles, discusses his new documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' at Concord Carlisle Regional High School.
The documentary, airing on PBS March 30 and 31, features narration by George Clooney and voices by Jeff Goldblum and Meryl Streep.
Henley founded The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to preserve land associated with Thoreau after learning of planned commercial developments.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics attended a friendly match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium on Thursday.
France won the match 2-1, with Kylian Mbappe scoring a goal; Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez swapped jerseys with Mbappe.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla noted parallels between basketball and soccer, highlighting possession and counterattacks.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored a game-high 36 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-102 win over the Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists despite struggling with his shooting accuracy.
Celtics benefited from a controversial call against Nickeil Alexander-Walker in the game's final minutes.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics this season after rehabbing a ruptured Achilles' tendon.
Tatum's decision to return was influenced by the Celtics' strong position in the championship race.
Tatum is averaging 9.2 rebounds per game but struggling with shooting, below career averages in field goals and 3-pointers.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard, Friday afternoon.
The shooting followed an altercation between two armed men who reportedly knew each other, officials said.
The Middlesex District Attorney's Office and police are actively investigating the incident; no public threat remains.
